raw.title: "World first" Vertical Floating Solar Technology Plant Begins Operation in Bavarian gravel pit
raw.excerpt:
I'm not certain they actually are the first globally, but it's a lovely project. Bavaria isn't far enough north that east-west vertical bifacial panels outperform flat solar in net power output, but there is already a huge amount of solar in the German grid producing at midday. For a commercial volt farm there's sometimes more money in serving the undersupplied morning and evening times, when the sun is low.

Go much further north and with big northern daylight swings seasonally, vertical bifacials can actually outperform regular south facing panels! They are also pretty resistant against hail and wind storms!

submitted by /u/cromlyngames
[link] [comments]

llm_processed.summary: A vertical floating solar plant in Bavaria demonstrates innovative solar technology that optimizes energy production and resists weather damage, offering a scalable renewable energy solution.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: Bridges solar technology with flood-resilient infrastructure design for climate adaptation.
